steps.title: Use the discriminant
steps.content:
For the equation $-x^2+bx-81=0$, the discriminant is
$$
b^2-4(-1)(-81)=b^2-324
$$
No real solutions means the discriminant is less than $0$.

steps.title: Find the integer range
steps.content:
Apply the condition:
$$
b^2-324<0
$$
$$
b^2<324
$$
$$
|b|<18
$$
Since $b$ is an integer, the possible values run from $-17$ through $17$.

steps.title: Choose the least value
steps.content: The least integer in that range is $-17$. The answer is -17.
